settings.clear_cache_desc: This will remove local logs and temporary data. Your vault data will NOT be deleted.

biometric_modal.title: Enable Biometric Unlock
biometric_modal.description: Enter your master password to securely store it in your device's keychain.

export.title: Export Vault
export.warning_desc: You are about to export your passwords in plain text. Anyone with this file will be able to see your credentials. Store it securely.
export.attachment_warning: Note: Attachment files will not be exported.

import.title: Import Vault
import.drag_drop: Drag & Drop CSV/JSON here or Click to Select
import.note_desc: Duplicate entries will be added as new items with a timestamp tag. No existing data will be overwritten.

reset_modal.title: Forgot Master Password?
reset_modal.warning_desc: EtherVault is a zero-knowledge, local-first password manager. We do not store your master password on any servers. If you forget it, we cannot recover or reset it for you. ALL credentials stored in this local vault will be permanently lost if you reset.
reset_modal.step1: Go to your device's System Settings.
reset_modal.step3: Tap 'Clear Storage' or 'Clear Data' to delete the database.
reset_modal.step4: Relaunch the app to set up a new master password.
reset_modal.android_step1: Go to your device's System Settings.
reset_modal.android_step3: Tap 'Clear Storage' or 'Clear Data' to delete the database.
reset_modal.android_step4: Relaunch the app to set up a new master password.
reset_modal.ios_step1: Press and hold the EtherVault icon on your Home Screen.
reset_modal.ios_step2: Tap 'Remove App' then 'Delete App' to uninstall and wipe all local storage.
reset_modal.ios_step3: Reinstall EtherVault from the App Store.
reset_modal.ios_step4: Relaunch the app to set up a new master password.
reset_modal.desktop_step2: Go to the AppData folder: macOS: '~/Library/Application Support/EtherVault', Windows: '%APPDATA%\EtherVault'.
reset_modal.desktop_step3: Delete the entire 'EtherVault' folder to clear database and settings.
reset_modal.desktop_step4: Relaunch the app to set up a new master password.
reset_modal.web_step2: Go to the Application or Storage tab.
reset_modal.web_step3: Select IndexedDB, find 'EtherVaultDB', and click 'Delete database', then clear Local Storage.
reset_modal.web_step4: Reload the webpage to set up a new master password.
reset_modal.confirm_reset: WARNING: This will permanently delete all your credentials stored in this app and reset the app. This cannot be undone! Are you absolutely sure?
reset_modal.reset_failed: Automatic reset failed. Please use the manual steps instead.

found_modal.warning_text: Once imported, you must log in with the password used on the cloud vault. If you forgot the cloud password, select Cancel now. Check our Help/FAQ on how to clear cloud data to start a new sync.

about.title: About

about.privacy_desc: Your data is encrypted with AES-256-GCM purely on your device. We do not collect any personal data or analytics.

faq_view.title: Frequently Asked Questions

1.title: 1. Master Password Security
1.desc: EtherVault is a zero-knowledge, local-first password manager. We do not store your master password on any servers. If you forget it, we cannot recover or reset it for you. Please make sure you have memorized your master password.

2.title: 2. Cloud Sync & Data Privacy
2.access_desc: EtherVault uses a dedicated sandboxed folder for syncing. We do not have, and cannot request, permission to access any other files, folders, or personal data stored in your cloud drive.
2.view_data_title: How to View App Data in Google Drive
2.view_data_desc: On Google Drive, EtherVault stores encrypted files in a hidden AppData folder to prevent accidental deletion. To view or manage it, go to Google Drive Web > Settings (gear icon) > Settings > Manage Apps. You will see EtherVault listed under your authorized applications.

3.title: 3. Forgot Cloud Password & Clearing Sync Data
3.desc: Because EtherVault is a zero-knowledge password manager, we cannot recover your master password. If you forgot the cloud password, the cloud backup cannot be decrypted. You must clear the cloud data to start syncing again. Choose one of the following methods:
3.method1_title: Method 1: Manually Delete via Google Drive Settings
method1_steps: Open Google Drive in your web browser.
Click the gear icon in the top right and select Settings.
Click Manage Apps in the left sidebar.
Locate EtherVault, click Options, and select Delete hidden app data.
3.method2_title: Method 2: Force Overwrite via Sync Conflict
method2_steps: Ensure you have at least one credential entry in your local vault (if empty, create a temporary one).
Go to Settings > Sync Provider, and reconnect your cloud provider.
The app will detect different keys and display the 'Sync Conflict Detected' window.
Select 'Use Local' to overwrite the cloud vault with your current local password and settings.

faq_view.footer: EtherVault Help Center — Keep your vault secure, always.

terms_view.title: Terms of Service
terms_view.intro: Welcome to EtherVault ("we", "our", or "the App"). By downloading, accessing, or using EtherVault, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use the App.

1.title: 1. Description of Service
1.desc: EtherVault is a cross-platform, local-first password management tool designed with a zero-knowledge architecture. It allows you to securely store, manage, and optionally synchronize your sensitive credentials (such as passwords, secure notes, and tags) across your devices.

2.title: 2. Zero-Knowledge Architecture & Your Responsibilities
2.master_password_desc: EtherVault is built on a zero-knowledge security model. Your Master Password is never sent to our servers, nor do we have any way to access, retrieve, or reset it. You are solely responsible for remembering your Master Password. If you lose or forget your Master Password, your encrypted data cannot be recovered by us or anyone else. We are not liable for any data loss resulting from a forgotten Master Password.
2.data_security_desc: All your data is encrypted locally on your device using AES-256 encryption. While we employ industry-standard security measures, you are responsible for maintaining the physical and logical security of your own devices (e.g., using device passcodes, enabling biometric authentication, and protecting against malware).

3.title: 3. Cloud Synchronization
3.desc: EtherVault offers optional cloud synchronization using your personal third-party cloud storage providers (such as Google Drive, iCloud, or OneDrive).
points: We do not host your synchronized data.
By enabling this feature, you agree to the Terms of Service and Privacy Policies of your chosen cloud storage provider.
All data is encrypted locally before being uploaded to your cloud provider.

4.title: 4. Premium Subscriptions & Purchases
4.desc: EtherVault may offer optional Premium features through In-App Purchases or third-party payment processors (e.g., Apple App Store, Google Play, Stripe, or Lemon Squeezy).
points: Anonymous Binding: EtherVault does not require a centralized user account. Premium subscriptions are tied to your device's anonymous ID and your respective app store account (Apple ID or Google Account).
Restoring Purchases: You can migrate your Premium status to new devices using the "Restore Purchases" feature, provided you are logged into the same app store account used for the original purchase.
Refunds: Refund policies are governed by the respective app store or payment processor where the purchase was made.

5.title: 5. Privacy
5.desc: Your privacy is fundamentally important to us. Please review our Privacy Policy to understand how we handle (and explicitly do not collect) your data.

6.title: 6. Open Source & License
6.desc: The source code for EtherVault is licensed under the MIT License. You are free to use, modify, and distribute the code in accordance with the terms of the MIT License. However, these Terms of Service govern your use of the official EtherVault application distributed by us through app stores or our official website.

7.title: 7. Limitation of Liability
7.desc: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, ETHERVAULT AND ITS DEVELOPERS SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, OR ANY LOSS OF PROFITS OR REVENUES, WHETHER INCURRED DIRECTLY OR INDIRECTLY, OR ANY LOSS OF DATA, USE, GOODWILL, OR OTHER INTANGIBLE LOSSES, RESULTING FROM: (A) YOUR ACCESS TO OR USE OF OR INABILITY TO ACCESS OR USE THE APP; (B) ANY UNAUTHORIZED ACCESS TO OR USE OF YOUR DEVICE OR YOUR ENCRYPTED DATA; (C) THE LOSS OF YOUR MASTER PASSWORD OR CORRUPTION OF YOUR VAULT. THE APP IS PROVIDED "AS IS" WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND.

8.title: 8. Modifications to the Terms
8.desc: We reserve the right to modify these Terms of Service at any time. We will notify users of any significant changes by updating the "Last Updated" date at the top of this document or via in-app notifications. Continued use of the App after any changes constitutes your acceptance of the new Terms.

9.title: 9. Contact Us
9.desc: If you have any questions about these Terms of Service, please contact us via our official GitHub repository.

privacy_view.title: Privacy Policy
privacy_view.intro: EtherVault ("we", "our", or "the App") is an open-source, privacy-first password manager designed to respect and protect your digital privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how EtherVault handles your data.
privacy_view.summary: In short: We do not collect, store, or sell your personal data. You own your data.

1.title: 1. Data Collection and Storage
1.local_desc: EtherVault operates on a "Local-First" basis. All your sensitive data (usernames, passwords, notes, tags) is stored securely on your local device (computer or mobile phone). This data is encrypted using military-grade AES-256 encryption.
1.server_desc: We do not operate a central server to store your vault data. We (the developers of EtherVault) have no access to your master password, your stored credentials, or your encryption keys. If you lose your Master Password, we cannot recover your data for you.

2.title: 2. Cloud Synchronization
2.desc: EtherVault offers an optional feature to synchronize your data across devices using your own personal cloud storage providers (currently Google Drive).
points: Encryption Before Sync: All data is encrypted locally on your device BEFORE being uploaded to the cloud.
User Control: Synchronization is optional and must be explicitly enabled and authenticated by you.
Third-Party Policies: Data stored on your cloud provider is subject to that provider's privacy policy and terms of service.

3.title: 3. Biometric Data
3.desc: EtherVault supports unlocking the application using biometric authentication (Fingerprint, TouchID, FaceID).
points: Local Processing: Biometric verification is handled entirely by your device's operating system.
No Access: EtherVault receives only a "pass/fail" confirmation from the OS. We do not access, collect, store, or transmit your actual fingerprint or facial data.

4.title: 4. Third-Party Requests
4.desc: To improve the user experience, EtherVault may automatically fetch icons (favicons) for the websites you store in your vault.
points: These requests may be sent directly to the target website or via a privacy-respecting favicon service.
These requests may technically expose your IP address to the content provider, similar to visiting the website in a browser.

5.title: 5. Device Permissions
lists: Network/Internet Access: Required for Cloud Synchronization and fetching website icons.
Biometric Hardware: Required to enable Fingerprint/FaceID/TouchID unlock.
Storage/File System: Required to store your encrypted vault files locally and for Import/Export functionality.

6.title: 6. Logs & Analytics
lists: Crash Reports: The App does not automatically send crash reports to us.
Local Logs: Usage logs are stored locally on your device for your own auditing and debugging purposes. These logs never leave your device unless you manually export and share them.

7.title: 7. Changes to This Policy
7.desc: We may update our Privacy Policy from time to time. Since the App is open source and local-first, significant changes to data handling would typically require a software update.

8.title: 8. Contact Us
8.desc: If you have any questions or suggestions about our Privacy Policy, you can reach out via our <0>GitHub</0> repository or project page.

privacy_view.footer: EtherVault — Your Code, Your Data, Your Vault.
